summary.intent: Address Codex automated review findings on PR #672 in the ars-ui repo, push fixes, and get the PR to a clean Codex-approved state with all CI checks passing.
summary.outcome: All five original Codex review threads were resolved and replied to. A follow-up commit (0632b021) was pushed. After multiple re-trigger attempts, Codex produced two additional findings (submenu pointer-leave highlight preservation and aria-keyshortcuts propagation), which were fixed with targeted tests, committed, and pushed.

repo: Menu component logic lives in crates/ars-components/src/selection/menu/mod.rs; submenu open-state interacts with pointer-leave highlight behavior.
aria-keyshortcuts must be propagated through the shared item base builder for checkbox, radio, and submenu item variants.
The project uses @codex review comment triggers on GitHub PRs; Codex acknowledges with 👀 and resolves with 👍 or new threads.
CI matrix includes Unit Tests, Release Verification, Coverage, and Dioxus feature-flag jobs; the last three tend to finish latest.

code.finding: Pointer-leave handler was not gating on submenu-open state, causing parent highlight to clear while a submenu was active. Fixed by making pointer-leave a no-op when a submenu is open.
aria-keyshortcuts attribute was not being forwarded through the shared item base builder to checkbox/radio/submenu item variants; fix centralized it there.

workflow: Codex re-trigger via @codex review comment is unreliable: the second trigger received no reaction after the full polling window and required a third attempt before Codex acknowledged with 👀.
Polling Codex state requires checking both comment reactions and GraphQL reviewThreads; reaction alone is insufficient.
Committing with 'Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>' trailer is the expected co-author convention for Codex-driven fixes in this repo.
cargo +nightly fmt --all --check and cargo clippy -p ars-components --all-targets -- -D warnings are the standard pre-commit gates.

friction: Codex did not acknowledge the second @codex review trigger at all within the normal window, requiring a third trigger — wasted ~10 minutes of polling.
Long CI matrix (Release Verification, Coverage, feature-flag jobs) meant multiple extended sleep-poll cycles before a clean green state.
No direct signal when Codex transitions from 👀 to finished; must poll both reactions and thread state repeatedly.
open_items: Verify that CI (Release Verification and Coverage jobs) reached a fully green state after the final push — transcript ends before those jobs completed.
Confirm Codex issued a final 👍 or LGTM review on commit after the third trigger — transcript ends while Codex still showed 👀.
